description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone block with a weathered, uneven surface and visible chisel or tool marks running diagonally across one face. The stone has a two-tone coloration, with a darker grayish upper layer and a lighter, sandy beige lower portion. One side of the block features handwritten black ink or paint markings, likely cataloging or identification numbers, suggesting it may have been part of an archaeological or architectural collection. The edges are irregular and chipped, indicating age or exposure to the elements. The block appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban or historical context.
description: A weathered, two-tone stone block with diagonal tool marks and cataloging inscriptions, featuring chipped edges and a solid, dense appearance.
description_view_0: The main face shows diagonal chisel marks on a gray surface, with a clear division between the darker top and lighter bottom.
description_view_1: A narrow side profile reveals the block's thickness and the rough, uneven texture of the stone.
description_view_2: The reverse side displays handwritten cataloging numbers and letters in black, with a smoother surface and less visible tool marks.
description_view_3: Another narrow side profile, similar to view 1, showing the irregular, chipped edge and layered coloration.
attribute_location_context: historic urban site or archaeological feature
attribute_weathering_pattern: Chipped corners and edges with surface abrasions; tool marks remain visible, indicating moderate weathering but not extensive erosion.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Handwritten cataloging numbers and letters in black ink or paint are visible on one face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely to be stacked or aligned with other similar blocks as part of a larger structure or barrier.
